echo.echo_builder module

class echo.echo_builder.Network

Bases: object

A class for holding our network dict

add_asset_dict(node_id: str, node_type: NodeType, ports: list, units: Optional[Units] = None, node_data: Optional[str] = None)
add_battery_node(node_id, ports: list, param_dict: Optional[dict] = None)
add_data_node(node_id: str, node_type: NodeType, node_data: Union[str, ArrayType], ports: list, units: Units)
add_edge(node_tuple: tuple, port_tuple: tuple, res=None, edge_name: Optional[str] = None)
add_edge_no_ports(edge_name, node_tuple, res)
add_flex_node(node_id: str, ports: list, units: Units)
add_node_parameters(node_id: str, param_dict: dict)
add_objective(obj_name: str, obj_type: str, component: Optional[dict] = None, prices: Optional[ArrayType] = None)
add_port_to_node(node_id: str, port_name: str)
add_tellegen_node(node_id: str, ports: list, units: Units)
components = {}
create_tellegen_network_from_dict(dict)
edges = {}
name: Optional[str] = 'default_name'
objectives = {}
to_dict()
class echo.echo_builder.NetworkSet(description, name='default_name')

Bases: object

A network set is a grouping of separate network pieces that we want to be able to optimise/analyse as a group. The networks in a network set must have the same interval duration and time periods. This class can be used to manage single networks too, if useful.

add_network(network: dict)

Add a single network to the network set.

add_networks_from_list(networks: list)

Add a list of networks to the network set.

export_results_to_df()

Exports optimisation results to a pandas dataframe and returns that dataframe

get_echo_model(network_name: str)

Returns an echo model of a network in the network set

get_echo_optimiser(network_name: str)

Returns the echo optimiser for a network in the network set

optimise_network_set(log_file=None, verbose=False)

Optimises the set of networks defined in the class instance.

to_df(node, port)

for each network segment in netset, gets the value of port on node, if that node and port combination does not exist sets the result to nan. :param node: name of node to look at :param port: port to get value of :return: dataframe with timeseries data

echo.echo_builder.append_results(result_dict, network_dict, in_place=False)

Takes dict of results from an echo model, and appends them to the correct places in a network dict.

echo.echo_builder.array_length_check(array, length, message, scalar_ok=False)
echo.echo_builder.check_nx_for_floating_nodes(g)

Checks if we have nodes without any edge

echo.echo_builder.check_port_names_are_consistent(g)
echo.echo_builder.combine_two_graphs(g1, g2, p1=None, p2=None)

Takes two networkx graphs and combines them, returning the combination as a third graph

echo.echo_builder.connect_nodes(system, node1, node2, port1: str, port2: str)

Connects two nodes together via specified ports. Doesn’t currently handle blank port names. :param system: echo graph :param node1: node on edge :param node2: other node on edge :param port1: port on edge :param port2: other port on edge

echo.echo_builder.construct_echo_node(system, node_name_dict: dict, node, node_dict: dict, df: DataFrame)

Does logic for working out which type of node to build. Builds the node, adds to the echo graph, and updates the node name dict

echo.echo_builder.convert_dict_to_nx(netw_jsn: dict, verbose: bool = True)

Creates nx graph from network dictionary

echo.echo_builder.convert_nx_to_echo(g, df, verbose=True)

Creates echo model from nx graph

echo.echo_builder.convert_objective_to_echo_objective(em, node_name_dict: dict, objective_dict: dict, verbose: bool = True)

Converts all the objectives defined in an objective set to echo objectives, and returns an echo objective set.

echo.echo_builder.create_battery_node(node_dict)

Creates an echo battery node from the provided node dict.

echo.echo_builder.create_boiler(boiler_dict, df)
echo.echo_builder.create_chiller_node(chiller_dict, df)
echo.echo_builder.create_demand_tariff(tariff_dict, node_name_dict, em)

Creates an echo demand tariff from a tariff dictionary

echo.echo_builder.create_ev(ev_dict, df)
echo.echo_builder.create_export_tariff(tariff_dict, node_name_dict, em)

Creates an echo export tariff from a tariff dictionary

echo.echo_builder.create_flex_node(node_dict)

Creates an echo flexible node from the provided node dict. A flexible node is a node with a single flexible port with a specified unit.

echo.echo_builder.create_import_tariff(tariff_dict, node_name_dict, em)

Creates an echo import tariff from a tariff dictionary

echo.echo_builder.create_inverter_node(inverter_dict)
echo.echo_builder.create_load_node(load_dict, df)
echo.echo_builder.create_peak_power_objective(tariff_dict, node_name_dict, em)

Creates an echo peak power objective from an objective dictionary

echo.echo_builder.create_quadratic_objective(tariff_dict, node_name_dict, em)

Creates an echo quadratic objective from an objective dictionary

echo.echo_builder.create_solar_node(solar_dict, df)
echo.echo_builder.create_tellegen_node(node_dict)

Creates an echo tellegen node from the provided node dict.

echo.echo_builder.create_throughput_tariff(tariff_dict, node_name_dict, em)

Creates an echo throughput tariff from a tariff dictionary

echo.echo_builder.extract_results(optimiser, node_name_dict: dict, results_key: Optional[dict] = None)

Extracts results from an echo model and returns them in a dict. Results key arg allows user to specify which results they want returned.

echo.echo_builder.get_echo_port_units(node_name, port_unit)
echo.echo_builder.get_pyomo_var_map(optimiser)
echo.echo_builder.get_pyomo_vars_from_port_name(port_name, var_map)
echo.echo_builder.get_tariff_component_from_node_port_name(tariff_dict, node_name_dict, em)

Retrieves an objective component defined in an objective dict from an echo model and returns it.

echo.echo_builder.get_vals_from_df(df, col_name)
echo.echo_builder.port_connectivity_check(port_obj, graph)
echo.echo_builder.process_field(field, df)

Checks if a field points to data in a df or if it contains the data directly.

echo.echo_builder.process_single_network(network_dict: dict, interval_duration: int, time_periods: int, verbose: bool = True)

Ingests a single network in a networkset, converts it to an echo model, runs the optimiser, appends results to the original network, and returns results.

echo.echo_builder.retrieve_key(d, val)
echo.echo_builder.retrieve_value(d, key)
echo.echo_builder.run_echo_optimiser(echo_graph, objective_set, interval_duration, time_periods, expansion_periods=1, discount_rate=0, optimiser_engine='cplex', opt_display=False, verbose=True)

Runs the echo optimiser on an echo graph with an echo objective set. Returns the optimiser object.
